creator: durian@shadetreesoftware.com
text:
Riggs pointed out that I forgot to attach the port code.  Oops.

mike

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	linux-eagle5
#	linux-eagle5/files
#	linux-eagle5/files/eagle.sh
#	linux-eagle5/files/pkg-deinstall.in
#	linux-eagle5/distinfo
#	linux-eagle5/pkg-descr
#	linux-eagle5/pkg-message
#	linux-eagle5/pkg-plist
#	linux-eagle5/Makefile
#
echo c - linux-eagle5
mkdir -p linux-eagle5 > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - linux-eagle5/files
mkdir -p linux-eagle5/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - linux-eagle5/files/eagle.sh
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/files/eagle.sh 
<< 'aab3874daff30260fef4b4e896881c7e'
X#!/bin/sh
X#
X# $FreeBSD:$
X
XEAGLEBIN=%%DATADIR%%/bin/eagle
X
X$EAGLEBIN "$@"
aab3874daff30260fef4b4e896881c7e
echo x - linux-eagle5/files/pkg-deinstall.in
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/files/pkg-deinstall.in 
<< 'bbfdf219a882807810e11c87a6e32721'
X#!/bin/sh
X
XPATH=/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin
X
XDATADIR="%%DATADIR%%"
X
Xpost-deinstall() {
X	echo "WARNING: If you will *NOT* use this package anymore, please remote 
the"
X	echo " following file manually:"
X	echo " ${DATADIR}/bin/eagle.key"
X	echo
X}
X
Xcase $2 in
X	POST-DEINSTALL)
X		post-deinstall
X		;;
Xesac
bbfdf219a882807810e11c87a6e32721
echo x - linux-eagle5/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/distinfo << '62ab34d4c21168892a7565986bfd00fa'
XMD5 (eagle-lin-5.5.0.run) = 5583ff7885f1f1530511cf1c799737c9
XSHA256 (eagle-lin-5.5.0.run) = 
730d089351aaa11176f32050b4a11bd7f46ee80cabef570a8de64c9e5822fcdc
XSIZE (eagle-lin-5.5.0.run) = 26597130
62ab34d4c21168892a7565986bfd00fa
echo x - linux-eagle5/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/pkg-descr << '37a003c20931ea1105739e03d44c5e8c'
XThe EAGLE Layout Editor is an easy to use, yet powerful tool for designing
Xprinted circuit boards (PCBs). The name EAGLE is an acronym, which stands for
XEasily Applicable Graphical Layout Editor.
X
XThe program consists of three main modules:
X
Xo Layout Editor
Xo Schematic Editor
Xo Autorouter
X
Xwhich are embedded in a single user interface. Therefore there is no need for
Xconverting netlists between schematics and layouts.
X
XThis is a Light Freeware Edition. It has the following limitations:
X
Xo The useable board area is limited to 100 x 80 mm (4 x 3.2 inches).
Xo Only two signal layers can be used (Top and Bottom).
Xo The schematic editor can only create one sheet.
Xo Support is only available via email or through our forum (no fax or phone
X  support).
Xo Use is limited to non-profit applications or evaluation purposes.
X
XApart from these limitiations the EAGLE Light Edition can do anything the
XProfessional Edition can do. You can even load, view and print drawings that
Xexceed these limits!
X
XWWW: http://www.cadsoftusa.com
37a003c20931ea1105739e03d44c5e8c
echo x - linux-eagle5/pkg-message
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/pkg-message << '641efb2c54bf828695494cbcedb9436c'
XEagle needs to be run as "root" first, in order to have write access 
Xto %%PREFIX%%/share/eagle to write a program key file.
X
XOnce this has been done, you can delete the "eagle" directory in your 
X/root directory, which is created during the initially startup, and 
Xrun it as a normal user.
X
XIt is entirely possible that you need linux 2.6 emulation support
Xin the kernel.  The /usr/ports/UPDATING entry on 20080318 has
Xgood instructions on how to run linux 2.6.16 kernel emulation.
641efb2c54bf828695494cbcedb9436c
echo x - linux-eagle5/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/pkg-plist << '84307597d21521f51a3d8578b1756d0e'

sed 's/^X//' >linux-eagle5/Makefile << '0027c307f947e266fe736241a6a4d2bd'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	linux-eagle5
X# Date created:				20 March, 2009
X# Whom:					Michael Durian <durian@shadetreesoftware.com>
X#
X# $FreeBSD:$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	linux-eagle5
XEAGLEBASEVERSION?=	5.5
XEAGLEBUGFIX?=	0
XEAGLEVERSION=	${EAGLEBASEVERSION}.${EAGLEBUGFIX}
XPORTVERSION=	${EAGLEVERSION:S/r/./}
XPORTREVISION?=	0
XCATEGORIES?=	cad linux
XMASTER_SITES=	ftp://ftp.cadsoft.de/eagle/program/${EAGLEBASEVERSION}/
XDISTNAME=	eagle-${EAGLEVERSION}
XDISTFILES=	eagle-lin-${EAGLEVERSION}.run
X
XMAINTAINER?=	durian@shadetreesoftware.com
XCOMMENT?=	An easy to use, yet powerful tool for designing printed circuit 
boards
X
XONLY_FOR_ARCHS=	i386 amd64
X
XDATADIR=	${PREFIX}/share/${PORTNAME:S/linux-//}
XEXTRACT_CMD=	${SED}
XEXTRACT_BEFORE_ARGS=	-e '1,/^__DATA__$$/d'
XEXTRACT_AFTER_ARGS=	| ${BZCAT} -c 2>/dev/null | ${TAR} -xf -
X
XRESTRICTED=	Usage permitted for non-commercial purposes only
XNO_CDROM=	Sale for profit is not permitted
X
XUSE_LINUX=	yes
XUSE_XLIB=	yes
XNO_BUILD=	yes
XPKGMESSAGE=	${WRKDIR}/pkg-message
XSUB_FILES=	pkg-deinstall
X
XMAN1=		eagle5.1
X
XCPIO?=		/usr/bin/cpio --quiet -pdum -R
X
Xdo-install:
X	@${MKDIR} ${DATADIR}
X	@ cd ${WRKSRC} && ${FIND} * -type f -print | ${CPIO} ${BINOWN}:${BINGRP} 
${DATADIR}
X	@ cd ${DATADIR} && ${FIND} * -type d -print | ${XARGS} ${CHMOD} 0755
X	@${INSTALL_MAN} ${DATADIR}/doc/eagle.1 ${PREFIX}/man/man1/eagle5.1
X	@${SED} -e "s:%%DATADIR%%:${DATADIR}:g" ${FILESDIR}/eagle.sh > 
${PREFIX}/bin/eagle5
X	@${CHMOD} 0555 ${PREFIX}/bin/eagle5
X
Xpost-install:
X	@${SED} -e "s:%%PREFIX%%:${PREFIX}:g" ${PKGDIR}/pkg-message > \
X		${PKGMESSAGE}
X.if !defined(BATCH)
X	@${ECHO_MSG}
X	@${CAT} ${PKGMESSAGE}
X	@${ECHO_MSG}
X.endif
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
0027c307f947e266fe736241a6a4d2bd
exit
